figures.caption: Figure 3: Performance and memory usage across different backbones and anomaly detection algorithms. The numeric values indicate the maximum performance scores achieved across the considered strategies, while the color scale represents memory consumption (MB) on a logarithmic scale. Darker blue tones correspond to higher memory requirements, predominantly seen in WResNet50, while lighter shades highlight the efficiency of edge-optimized models like MCUNet. FT indicates the Fine-Tuning strategy, JT the joint training. For PatchCore, ”cl 10k” indicates the PatchcoreCL method with a memory bank of 10k samples, and ”clpp 10k” indicates the Patchcore++ variant with a memory bank of 10k. For PaDiM and PaDiM-Lite the ”cl multi” and ”cl uni” indicates the multimodal and unimodal variants. For the rest of the methods the ”cl 40” indicate replay with a memory buffer of 40 samples.
Table 4: Benchmark results on ViSA across all model–backbone combinations in the continual learning setting. Results are reported as: Image F1 / Pixel F1 / GFLOPs / Memory (MB).

Figure 5: Evaluation of VAD models across different backbones on the MVTec Dataset. The charts compare F1 Pixel Level, F1 Image Level, computational complexity (GFLOPS), and Memory (MB). The results highlight the trade-offs between detection accuracy and hardware efficiency, with MCUNet (blue), MobileNet (orange), and PhiNet (green) evaluated across various state-of-the-art methods.

Figure 1: Continual Learning setting for the VAD. Each task introduces a new item, the VAD model must identify anomalous products at the image level and localize defects at the pixel level for newly introduced items, while maintaining strong performance on previously learned categories.
Table 2: Benchmark results on MVTec AD across all model–backbone combinations in the continual learning setting. Results are reported as: Image F1 / Pixel F1 / GFLOPs / Memory (MB).

Figure 4: Comparison of the considered methods across tasks in the CL setting using Mobilenet as backbone and MVTec as dataset. For the Dinomaly model, the considered backbone is DeiT Tiny. In the PatchCore subplot, “CL” denotes PatchCoreCL and “CLPP” denotes PatchCoreCL++, each followed by the memory bank size.

Figure 2: Memory-Performance trade-off analysis. Scatter plots illustrating the relationship between Memory usage (log scale) and detection accuracy (F1 score) at the pixel level (a) and image level (b). Different markers represent the backbone architectures, while colors identify the specific anomaly detection (AD) models. Marker size correlates with computational complexity in FLOPS, providing a comprehensive view of model efficiency and scalability for resource-constrained environments.

Figure 6: Comparison of the considered methods across tasks in the CL setting using Mobilenet as backbone and VisA as dataset. For the Dinomaly model, the considered backbone is DeiT Tiny. In the PatchCore subplot, “CL” denotes PatchCoreCL and “CLPP” denotes PatchCoreCL++, each followed by the memory bank size.
